issue_title: Required options should become positional arguments
issue_body:
e.g. `globus transfer bookmark show --bookmark-id=<UUID>`
should become `globus transfer bookmark show <UUID>`.

Requires #73 for commands that deal with endpoints and paths to simplify the interface of some commands (e.g. `globus transfer async-transfer`, which without any simplification would require four positional arguments instead of two).

issue_title: Add project-wide logging using typical python loggers
issue_body:
The entire SDK should be logging things using python logging tools.

That way, people can turn on log levels and attach handlers to the logger named `globus_sdk`, and get good log output.

Important sites where this can be of use:
- DEBUG: loading tokens and config -- explain where a client got its token from at instantiation time
- DEBUG: request sent/received -- show full path requested + query params, log headers (redact auth header content other than type), received headers &c.
- ERROR: show captured requests exceptions that convert to `NetworkError`s

issue_title: Performance Optimizations
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

There is rudamentary full sync code in place and with that, we're finding performance issues.

- https://drive.google.com/open?id=1OwLcvS-ONGmgucL4Tccf5nqP2r_QCglv

The link above is the `cProfile` output which can be loaded using the [`pstats`](https://docs.python.org/3.5/library/profile.html) standard library module to see where time is spent.

### Known "low hanging fruit"

The following things are where we're seeing the *most* time spent which can likely be optimized.

- Remove support for python 2 from `pyrlp`
- Further `pyrlp` optimizations
- Caching of the `VMState.prev_hashes` generation.

issue_title: tornado should depend on certifi
issue_body: The tornado package on PyPI depends on [certifi](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/certifi). The tornado conda package probably should, too. certifi is required to make HTTPS requests.

issue_title: Source Deactivate Causes Too Many Arguments
issue_body:
I did my research and saw that this type of error was typically caused by having a `set` keyword in setting an environment variable. However, I had none. Finally, it dawned on me that between July 2015 and March 2016 that the way to deactivate was changed from:

`source deactivate py34`

to simply

`source deactivate`

When did this happen and how/why is it not make backwards compatible by simply ignoring extra argument?

issue_title: HTTP 503 error when local http channel is queried after other channels
issue_body:
I've been receiving strange 503 errors when attempting to use simple conda commands (conda search) with multiple local channels configured. One is a "file" channel, the other a simple local http server. 

The channels, when used independently, work fine. 

The conda commands seem to hang, then fail, in the "session.get" in fetch.py's fetch_repodata. 

Observations: if I purposely order the requests such that our local http server comes first, it works fine. If ordered such that the http server request is not first, it fails. This is consistent with or without proxy, with or without cache=True, and with or without enabling concurrency in fetch_index. Also, when the operations fail, I see no indication that any requests even reach the webserver, so it seems to be a client-side failure.

It is always when the local http channel comes after some other channel (even defaults).

```
Current conda install:

               platform : linux-64
          conda version : 4.2.9

issue_title: [ACS] The Kubernetes get-credentials command overwrites local kubeconfig file
issue_body:
The `az acs kubernetes get-credentials` command is really useful, however, I noticed that it seems to overwrite the local kube config file (e.g. `~/.kube/config`), and I can't seem to find a way to have it simply add the new cluster/context/user info to my existing kube config file (unless I choose to do it myself and not use this command). It would be cool if there was a way for me to do something along the lines of any of the following:

1. Have the `get-credentials` command add to the default config file. This behavior is similar to how minikube seems to work when you initialize a development cluster. It auto-adds the cluster details to your config in a non-invasive way.

2. Provide a mode that simply outputs the necessary shell commands (e.g. `kubectl config set-cluster`) in order to augment the default config file, which allows you to eval it yourself, similarly to how `docker-machine env` works. #1 would presumably be an automated version of this.

3. Write the ACS-specific config file to a specified location on disk, so I could update my `KUBE_CONFIG` env var and merge together the multiple config files, or explicitly use the `--kubeconfig` flag when using the k8s CLI. Maybe the existing behavior could default to the global config path, but you could override it with a flag.

Maybe the existing behavior is fine in practice, but it would be cool to have the same simple experience of creating an ACS cluster and connecting to it, without affecting a user's existing config setting.

issue_title: Documentation reorganizing
issue_body:
aiohttp doc was started from very few pages with relative small content on each.
Now it grows.
Take a look on *Client Usage* for example: http://aiohttp.readthedocs.io/en/stable/client.html 
Endless list of topics in navigation bar and infinite page to scroll on the right.

The same is true for web server docs, the situation is even worse.

The proposal is:
1. Shrink navbar to top livel topics only: client, server, misc (the last is a subject for discussion).
2. Client should contain `Client Usage` and `Client Reference` but we need split the Usage into several pages like "Basics", "Cookie Support", "File Sending" etc. I don't know the splitting criteria, it is a hard choice -- but we need it.
3. The same for `Client Reference` but the splitting is more obvious: ClientSession, request and response, connectors and DNS resolvers.
4. Server part: Tutorial, Usage and Reference, Logging, Low-Level Server and Deployment
5. Others

@aio-libs/aiohttp-committers I need your suggestions and help

issue_title: Config validation
issue_body:
Charms need to be able to validate their configuration, going into a blocked state until the operator fixes invalid configuration.

charms.reactive should make this easy.

The PostgreSQL charm does this using an `@preflight` mechanism. The hook validates the configuration, and if it fails, sets the blocked state and does sys.exit(0). I do not think this is a good approach, because of the sys.exit(0) hack to abort the process.

You can't do this with standard reactive handlers, because during a config-changed hook handlers may attempt to run using invalid configuration before the config validation handler has had a chance to run.

I do not have an elegant solution to propose.

issue_title: xdist misidentifies failing test on teardown crash
issue_body:
Hi!

I'm seeing xdist flagging the wrong test when a crash happens at test teardown.
Given 2 tests:
- `test_a`, passes but crashes at teardown
- `test_b`, passes

xdist will identify `test_b` as failing.
I have been able to reproduce while using pytest-xdist and pytest-timeout.
Here are the steps I've been following:
- Test file:
```
import pytest
import time

@pytest.fixture
def timeout_fixture(request):
    def long_wait():
        time.sleep(20)
    request.addfinalizer(long_wait)

def test_a(timeout_fixture):
    print("hello")

def test_b():
    print("world")

def test_c():
    print("bonjour")

def test_d():
    print("monde")
```
- Run test with `--timeout-method=thread`, so pytest-timeout will kill our process and xdist will see it crashed:
```$ py.test -s -x -vv --timeout=2 --timeout-method=thread -n 1```

Result:
```
[...]
test_bug.py::test_a 
[gw0] PASSED test_bug.py::test_a 
+++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++ Timeout ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
[...]
[gw0] node down: Not properly terminated
[gw0] FAILED test_bug.py::test_b 
Replacing crashed slave gw0
[gw1] darwin Python 2.7.10 cwd: /private/tmp/test
[gw1] Python 2.7.10 (default, Jul 30 2016, 19:40:32)  -- [GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 8.0.0 (clang-800.0.34)]
gw1 [4]
test_bug.py::test_d 
[gw1] PASSED test_bug.py::test_d 
test_bug.py::test_c 
[gw1] PASSED test_bug.py::test_c 
========================================================================================================================================================== FAILURES ==========================================================================================================================================================
________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________ test_bug.py _________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
[gw0] darwin -- Python 2.7.10 /usr/bin/python
Slave 'gw0' crashed while running 'test_bug.py::test_b'
============================================================================================================================================= 1 failed, 3 passed in 4.01 seconds =============================================================================================================================================
```

`test_b` is seen as failing but hasn't been executed at all.

I believe this is due to the way xdist handles crashed items. When a node crashes, it pops the running item and reschedule it on a new node. However, when the node crashes at teardown, the running item has already been dequeued.

issue_title: letsencrypt-auto install didn't remove SSLCertificateChainFile statement
issue_body:
I'm using Apache on Debian. I already had TLS configured, and thus had `SSLCertificateFile`, `SSLCertificateKeyFile` and `SSLCertificateChainFile` statements in my Apache site config.

letsencrypt-auto correctly replaced the values of the first two, but failed to change `SSLCertificateChainFile`, resulting in breakage.

Invocation:

```
./letsencrypt-auto install --apache -d example.com -d www.example.com --cert-path /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/fullchain.pem --key-path /etc/letsencrypt/keys/0000_key-letsencrypt.pem --agree-dev-preview 
```

Site config before invocation:

```
<VirtualHost *:80>
        ServerName example.com
        ServerAlias www.example.com
        DocumentRoot ...

        ErrorLog ...
        CustomLog ...
</VirtualHost>

<VirtualHost *:443>
        ServerName example.com
        ServerAlias www.example.com
        DocumentRoot ...

        ErrorLog ...
        CustomLog ...

        SSLEngine on
        SSLCertificateFile ...
        SSLCertificateKeyFile ...
        SSLCertificateChainFile ...
</VirtualHost>
```

Apart from that, it worked nicely, and so we now finally have what CACert has kept promising for "next year" for the past 10 years or so. So thanks for all the work, letsencrypt is amazing! ❤

from marshmallow import Schema, fields

issue_body:
Hi,

New user of marshmallow here so please let me know if I'm missing something obvious. Is there a way to immediately stop deserializing and raise ValidationError if just 1 validation fail. issue_title: Improve documentation of `about gen`
issue_body:
Myself and others (https://github.com/nexB/aboutcode-manager/issues/238) have run into some confusion when trying to generate ABOUT files from an inventory file. 

Specifically, the default `MAPPING.CONFIG` values and file location were a mystery to me until consulting with @DennisClark.

https://github.com/nexB/aboutcode-toolkit/issues/305 Will go a long way to making this more clear as well.

issue_title: Resource.deserialize() ignores format parameter
issue_body:
Hi there,

I just found that Resources's deserialize() methods ignores the "format" parameter.
It instead tries to get the format from the requests's content-type (falling back to application/json).

```
 def deserialize(self, request, data, format='application/json'):
        """
        Given a request, data and a format, deserializes the given data.
        It relies on the request properly sending a ``CONTENT_TYPE`` header,
        falling back to ``application/json`` if not provided.
        Mostly a hook, this uses the ``Serializer`` from ``Resource._meta``.
        """
        deserialized = self._meta.serializer.deserialize(data, format=request.META.get('CONTENT_TYPE', 'application/json'))
        return deserialized
```
I would've sent in a pull req but I'm not sure which way to go - change 
```
format=request.META.get('CONTENT_TYPE', 'application/json')
```
to
```
format=request.META.get('CONTENT_TYPE', format)
```
or  drop the parameter from the method?

Cheers,
Patrick

issue_title: Z3Exception: Overflow encountered when expanding vector
Segmentation fault with single argument re.union
issue_body:
Sounds scarier than it actually is; I think it's more or less an arity issue:

```
(set-option :produce-models true)
(set-logic ALL)
(assert (str.in.re "" (re.union (str.to.re "A"))))
(check-sat)
```

This gives me a segmentation fault on Mac. I'm pretty sure it boils down to the fact that I *incorrectly* called `re.union` with just one argument. It might be nice to recognize and accept that as a degenerate case of course, but this should at least be syntactically rejected instead of getting a seg-fault on it.

Also I noticed `re.union` actually takes any number of arguments >= 2, though the documentation isn't very clear on that. Can you confirm?

issue_title: Add tab indexes to form elements
dropout layer failed to prevent upcasting
issue_body:
in layers/noise.py:

> # Using theano constant to prevent upcasting
> one = T.constant(1) 
> retain_prob = one - self.p

Actually, 'one' beomes a int16 and 'retain_prob' becomes float32 even if self.p is a float16 .
It should be like this:

> one = T.constant(1.0) 

Same is in updates.py

It's time to consider supporting float16 now.

issue_body:
An optional but probably nice-to-have keyword for records.

To be able to use: pgen export -f projects.yaml - build all projects with all tools which are supported by this repo or by pgen (if tools_supported is not defined, pgen will start building all projects with all tools which are supported - this is probably not often a case, as we are growing quite a number of tools ,and new tools soon will be added which are specific only for some MCU families (KDS, Simplicity..)).

The use would be:

```
# frdm-k64f blinky
common:
  tools_supported:
    - iar_arm
    - make_gcc_arm
```
```
# mbed-lpc1768 blinky
common:
  tools_supported:
    - uvision
    - coide
```

running pgen export -f projects.yaml should build k64f demo for iar_arm and gcc_arm, and lpc1768 dem for uvision and coide.

issue_title: Add 'Performance Tips' section to documentation
issue_labels: [{'id': 768587991, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw3Njg1ODc5OTE=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pytroll/satpy/labels/documentation', 'name': 'documentation', 'color': 'f9d0c4', 'default': False}, {'id': 751601587, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw3NTE2MDE1ODc=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pytroll/satpy/labels/good%20first%20issue', 'name': 'good first issue', 'color': '5319e7', 'default': True}, {'id': 323805265, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWwzMjM4MDUyNjU=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pytroll/satpy/labels/help%20wanted', 'name': 'help wanted', 'color': '159818', 'default': True}]
issue_body:
I've been trying to get a specific performance-intensive use case to work all day today and I've been trying various things to not run out of memory on my server. While the memory issues that I'm running in to are largely due to flaws in the MultiScene `save_animation` design I think it would be nice to have a page in the sphinx docs that talks about performance tips. It could combine various things from the dask and xarray docs including but not limited to:

1. Dask opportunistic caching: http://dask.pydata.org/en/latest/caching.html#automatic-opportunistic-caching
2. Set number of dask workers (threads)
3. Computing dask tasks/arrays all together (i.e. optimizing the dask graph between many objects)
3a. Not computing dask arrays more than needed. Running certain methods can compute the array before you want. For example, running `scn.show('my_ds')` will compute and show the data array, but will have to compute it again when `save_datasets` is called.
3b. Persist, compute, values
4. Dask ProgressBar
5. Dask profiling

issue_title: Tensorboard fails to add summaries without any warning
issue_body:
Hey guys I'm learning to use tensorboard and something wired happens. Tensorboard failed to add summary more than 10 times and showed no warning.Here is my code:
```
import tensorflow as tf
import numpy as np

def rmse(y, y_hat):
    """Compute root mean squared error"""
    return tf.sqrt(tf.reduce_mean(tf.square((y - y_hat))))

def forward(x, e):
    """Forward pass for our fuction"""
    # tensorflow has automatic broadcasting 
    # so we do not need to reshape e manually
    return tf.pow(x, e) 

n = 100 # number of examples
learning_rate = 5e-6

x = tf.placeholder(tf.float32)
y = tf.placeholder(tf.float32)

exp = tf.constant(2.0)
exp_hat = tf.Variable(4.0, name='exp_hat')

y_hat = forward(x, exp_hat)

loss = rmse(y, y_hat)
opt = tf.train.GradientDescentOptimizer(learning_rate)

loss_summary = tf.summary.scalar("loss", loss)
exp_summary = tf.summary.scalar("exp", exp_hat)
all_summaries = tf.summary.merge_all()

train_op = opt.minimize(loss) 

x_train = np.random.rand(n) + 10
y_train = x_train ** 2

loss_history = []
exp_history = []

with tf.Session() as sess:
    
    # Initialize all defined variables
    tf.global_variables_initializer().run()
    
    summary_writer = tf.summary.FileWriter('./tensorboard', sess.graph)
    
    for i in range(0, 500):
        print("Iteration %d" % i)
        summaries, curr_loss, curr_exp, _ = sess.run([all_summaries, loss, exp_hat, train_op], feed_dict={x: x_train, y: y_train})
        
        print("loss = %s" % curr_loss)
        print("exp = %s" % curr_exp)
        
        loss_history.append(curr_loss)
        exp_history.append(curr_exp)
        
        summary_writer.add_summary(summaries, i)
```

The code is from https://medium.com/towards-data-science/pytorch-vs-tensorflow-spotting-the-difference-25c75777377b

The result in console is:
`Iteration 8
loss = 2975.89
exp = 3.36951
Iteration 9
loss = 2721.11
exp = 3.33606`
The program stops here and would not go on without any messages. I did the debug and found it stopped at add_summary(). It's wired because even if I change log frequency to adding 1 summary every 10 iterations, it will still stop at the 90th iteration.
Is it a bug or something?

issue_title: Platform abstraction for slave SPI interface
issue_body:
We need a platform abstraction for a slave SPI interface. I present the following API for consideration and discussion:

```
/**
 * Indicates that a SPI transaction has completed with
 * the given length. The data written to the slave has been
 * written to the pointer indicated by the `input_ptr` argument
 * to the previous call to `otPlatSPISlavePrepareTransaction()`.
 *
 * If this function returns `true`, the transaction is cleared
 * and a subsequent call to `otPlatSPISlavePrepareTransaction()` is
 * required for the next transaction to proceed.
 *
 * If this function returns `false`, the previously prepared
 * transaction is kept intact.
 *
 * @param[in] len     Length of the completed transaction, in bytes.
 *
 * @retval `true`  The previously prepared transaction is to be
 *                 cleared and a subsequent call to
 *                 `otPlatSPISlavePrepareTransaction()` is required
 *                 for the next transaction to proceed.
 * @retval `false` The previously prepared transaction is kept
 *                 intact.
 */
bool otPlatSPISlaveTransactionComplete(uint16_t len);

/**
 * Initialize the SPI slave interface. Not fully ready until
 * `otPlatSPISlavePrepareTransaction()` is called. If
 * `otPlatSPISlavePrepareTransaction()` is not called before
 * the master begins a transaction, the resulting SPI transaction
 * should send all `0xFF` bytes.
 *
 * @retval ::kThreadError_None  Successfully enabled the SPI Slave interface.
 * @retval ::kThreadError_Fail  Failed to enable the SPI Slave interface.
 */
ThreadError otPlatSPISlaveEnable(void);

/**
 * Shutdown and disable the SPI slave interface.
 */
void otPlatSPISlaveDisable(void);

/**
 * Prepare data for the next transaction. Data pointers
 * MUST remain valid until `otPlatSPISlaveTransactionComplete()`
 * is called by the SPI slave driver.
 * 
 * @param[in] output_ptr  Data to be written to MISO pin
 * @param[in] input_ptr   Data to be read from MOSI pin
 * @param[in] max_len     Maximum transaction length, in bytes
 *
 * @retval ::kThreadError_None  Transaction was successfully prepared.
 * @retval ::kThreadError_Busy  A transaction is currently in progress.
 */
ThreadError otPlatSPISlavePrepareTransaction(
    const uint8_t *output_ptr,
    uint8_t *input_ptr,
    uint16_t max_len
);

/**
 * Returns true is a SPI transaction is in progress.
 * Returns false otherwise.
 */
bool otPlatSPISlaveTransactionInProgress(void);

/**
 * Signals to the SPI master that we want it to
 * initiate a SPI transaction. There is no error
 * condition. The request must be asserted for
 * after the call to otPlatSPISlavePrepareTransaction(). 
 */
void otPlatSPISlaveRequestTransaction(void);
```

issue_title: Suggestion:  HPKP key and config management
issue_body:
I would like to see a system where Certbot manages a pool of keys for HPKP deployment, as well as outputting config snippets to be "include"'ed into the main server config.
How I would think it should work:

On first run, the client should first display a warning regarding the risks of "bricking" a site due to poor HSTS deployment.
Upon agreement to the warning, the client should generate 3 RSA and/or EC keys, 1 to be deployed and 2 stand-by keys.
The client should obtain a certificate for the first "deployment" key and output the certificate set plus a configuration snippet for Apache and/or nginx to be "include"'ed into the server configuration, that will output public key pinnings for the deployed key and the two standby keys.
This should be per-domain, of course, like the certificate files.

On re-invocation of certbot, the client should consume one of the stand-by keys, converting it into a deployment key, and generate another stand-by key.  Along with the certificates output, should be a replacement set of HPKP header config files.

Perhaps a good starting point for the HPKP duration would be 90 days.

The number of stand-by keys and the HPKP duration should ideally be configurable, once again with the caveat that YMMV.

issue_title: Document Mnaul (DNS) challenge
issue_body:
Right now we suggest users use manual auth hooks to allow them to do the DNS challenge, and we even go so far as to suggest example scripts for them to run, but at no point do we explain what that challenge is doing!

There is already some things written about this in the manual plugin, which gets written out to the user when they call `certbot certonly --manual` without an auth hook flag. This command can give you information about any of our challenges, and you can specify which one by specifying that challenge with the --preferred-challenge flag.

Note that like the webroot challenge, certbot will tell the user to put certain files in certain directories to be served as part of the challenge. These files and directories are generated randomly for each run and shouldn't be put verbatim into the documentation. You can also look at these suggestions directly in the code: https://github.com/certbot/certbot/blob/master/certbot/plugins/manual.py

issue_title: Magic or API to allow skipping of cells during run all?
issue_body:
For reproducibility considerations, I usually rerun my notebook to make sure it executes correctly before I conclude an analysis. However, there are cases that I would like to ignore certain cells during re-execution, for example, cells that involves heavy computations or cells that produce different results each time.

I checked the [source code of run all](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/master/notebook/static/notebook/js/notebook.js#L2369) and it appears that all cells are executed, and there seems to be no way to stop [execution of cell](https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/master/notebook/static/notebook/js/codecell.js#L306). Actually in my own [SoS kernel](https://github.com/vatlab/SOS/) I implemented a [`%skip` magic](https://github.com/vatlab/SOS/blob/master/sos/jupyter/kernel.py#L1892) but the magic clears the output of the skipped cells because the cells are actually executed and returned immediately.

So my question is: Is there a  way to mark cells to exclude them from run-all? Or, would you consider a patch that skips cells with certain metadata so that kernels and extensions can make use of it to skip cells?

issue_title: UnresolvableObject during string interpolation.
issue_body:
Say,

```
input: remote('somefile')
output: "${input}.bak"
```

Interpolating `"${input}.bak"` raises `UnresolvableObject` and return untouched (`"${input}.bak"`), which confuses sos as it treats `${input}.bak` as a valid expanded filename. 

It would make sense to interpolate `${input}.bak` as `remote('${input}.bak')`, even `remote("somefile.bak")`.

The problem with returning a `remote` object from `interpolate` changes the type of return object and will cause many troubles later on. I guess it is better to pass the `UnresolvableObject` to the caller.

issue_title: Release resource for Python and Java worker
issue_body:
Currently, both Python and Java worker write a bunch of records into Redis but we don't have a mechanism to release them afterwards; in PR #2406 , we introduced a dictionary that maps from driver id to serialization context, but we won't delete the item after the driver exits or dies. 

In order to solve above issues, we propose some ideas here: [design doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UbCT23GDN1rZeCXY97HG6dM3jQ3xGU-Y0p-V8zuWrwE/edit?usp=sharing), please help to comment. Thanks in advance!

issue_title: Not possible to configure where Ray puts the Plasma store socket
issue_body:
### System information
- **OS Platform and Distribution (e.g., Linux Ubuntu 16.04)**: Ubuntu 17.10
- **Ray installed from (source or binary)**: source
- **Ray version**: 0.5.0
- **Python version**: 3.6.3

### Describe the problem

It seems it is currently always put under `"/tmp/plasma_store{}".format(random_name())`. This makes it hard to share the socket with others through Docker volume mounts.

For us one of big advantages of Ray is that it is using close-to-standard Arrow and Plasma to improve interoperability with other programs which might not be using Ray directly, but that we can still share memory objects with by asking them to just use Arrow and connect to our Plasma store (from another Docker container, on same machine, having a shared Docker volume mount or something similar; currently we are targeting a shared Docker volume mount). So in some way, our internal implementation of logic is done on top of Ray, but state sharing can be done (hopefully) just through Arrow and Plasma.

So, ideally we should be able to control where the socket is made, so that we can make sure it is made on a shared volume.

However, I have noticed that there are occasional "Unable to open URL" errors: (EOF occurred in violation of protocol (_ssl.c:645)).

### Expected / Actual behavior

If the webcam of a model is not on, streamlink should exit with a "No playable streams found on this URL":  

error: No playable streams found on this URL: https://chaturbate.com/little_mystery/

### Reproduction steps / Explicit stream URLs to test

[cli][info] Found matching plugin chaturbate for URL https://chaturbate.com/little_mystery/

issue_title: Add prefix search option for `get_datasets_for_location`
issue_body:
@jeremyh pointed out on slack that `get_datasets_for_location` will be affected by the recent changes to better support stacked datasets (see #415)

Previously we assumed that `dataset.location` points to a file, so reverse query from file to a set of stacked datasets worked by just finding all datasets that have locations with exact match.

https://github.com/opendatacube/datacube-core/blob/5f7211d2eb26ac10d6dfb754418d4bd3079cdf7a/datacube/drivers/postgres/_api.py#L252-L263

Now however every dataset will get a unique location uri, with fragment part (e.g. `file:///some/path/file.nc#part=0`, same applies for multi-doc yaml documents too). So to get the original behaviour back we need a version of that same query but with string prefix match instead of equality check. Any tools that uses this will need to be updated to use prefix search option.

Bonus: this would allow querying a folder as well.

issue_title: lstm - pytorch exported pb doesn't match specification
issue_body:
Use Netron open onnx/backend/test/data/pytorch-operator/test_operator_lstm/model.onnx can get:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6838753/39824565-a4bf96d2-53ea-11e8-87c5-edb132a839e7.png)

Here, output from first LSTM should have shape [seq_length, num_directions, batch_size, hidden_size] following the docs. (In pytorch, should have  (seq_len, batch, hidden_size * num_directions))
And then pass to next nodes [Shape, Gather, Unsqueeze ....] to get second LSTM's initial_h and initial_c which has shape [num_directions, batch_size, hidden_size].

Here is the problem,
normally, Gather should gather batch_size from output, and Concat with num_directions and hidden_size to obtain [num_directions, batch_size, hidden_size].
But pytorch export pb with index 1 of Gather and get num_directions because output shape changed from (seq_len, batch, hidden_size * num_directions) to [seq_length, num_directions, batch_size, hidden_size]. batch_size is no longer at index 1.
Finally get [num_directions, num_directions, hidden_size] to make a wrong initial_h and initial_c for second LSTM.

issue_title: File Readers: What kinds of formats are there?
issue_body:
This thread is for a living list of file formats for which to implement readers in PVGeo. The following are just a starting point and details for their development progress can be found in the [Readers Project Board](https://github.com/OpenGeoVis/PVGeo/projects/1).

**Please post file formats for which you would like to have a reader in ParaView and please include a link to some documentation/example files for that format.** 

Here are the file types we have already implemented:
- [x] GSLib Tables and SGeMS grids [Details](http://www.gslib.com/gslib_help/format.html)
- [x] Madagascar SSRSF [Details](http://www.ahay.org/wiki/RSF_Comprehensive_Description#Single-stream_RSF)
- [x] SEPLib binary data [Details]()
- [x] UBC Tensor Meshes (time series coming soon) [Details](http://giftoolscookbook.readthedocs.io/en/latest/content/fileFormats/format_index.html#meshes)
- [x] UBC OcTree Meshes (time series coming soon) [Details](http://giftoolscookbook.readthedocs.io/en/latest/content/fileFormats/meshOctreefile.html)
- [x] General delimited text and XYZ files

Here are some formats we are planning to implement:
- [ ] GIS Shapefiles Details [here](https://www.esri.com/library/whitepapers/pdfs/shapefile.pdf) and [here](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shapefile)
- [ ] ASCII Standard Well Logs [Details here](http://www.cwls.org/las/)
- [ ] ESRI Grids Details [here](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Esri_grid) and [here](http://desktop.arcgis.com/en/arcmap/10.3/manage-data/raster-and-images/esri-grid-format.htm)
- [ ] KMZ/KML [Details](https://developers.google.com/kml/documentation/kml_tut)
- [ ] Open Mining Format [Details](https://github.com/GMSGDataExchange/omf)

issue_title: Timestamps with microseconds *and* "Z" timezone are not parsed
issue_body:
christop...@gmail.com, 2010-12-08T14:56:06.000Z

Ultimately the whole datetime parsing should probably be reworked using e.g. http://pypi.python.org/pypi/isodate/0.4.0 or http://pypi.python.org/pypi/iso8601/
